title: Great Migrations
content_html:
Great Migrations

Animal migration, however it is defined, is far more than just the movement of animals. It can loosely be described as travel that takes place at regular intervals – often in an annual cycle – that may involve many members of a species, and is rewarded only after a long journey. It suggests inherited instinct. The biologist Hugh Dingle has identified five characteristics that apply, in varying degrees and combinations, to all migrations. They are prolonged movements that carry animals outside familiar habitats; they tend to be linear, not zigzaggy; they involve special behaviours concerning preparation (such as overfeeding) and arrival; they demand special allocations of energy. And one more: migrating animals maintain an intense attentiveness to the greater mission, which keeps them undistracted by temptations and undeterred by challenges that would turn other animals aside.

An arctic tern, on its 20,000 km flight from the extreme south of South America to the Arctic circle, will take no notice of a nice smelly herring offered from a bird-watcher’s boat along the way. While local gulls will dive voraciously for such handouts, the tern flies on. Why? The arctic tern resists distraction because it is driven at that moment by an instinctive sense of something we humans find admirable: larger purpose. In other words, it is determined to reach its destination. The bird senses that it can eat, rest and mate later. Right now it is totally focused on the journey; its undivided intent is arrival.

Reaching some gravelly coastline in the Arctic, upon which other arctic terns have converged, will serve its larger purpose as shaped by evolution: finding a place, a time, and a set of circumstances in which it can successfully hatch and rear offspring.

But migration is a complex issue, and biologists define it differently, depending in part on what sorts of animals they study. Joe! Berger, of the University of Montana, who works on the American pronghorn and other large terrestrial mammals, prefers what he calls a simple, practical definition suited to his beasts: ‘movements from a seasonal home area away to another home area and back again’. Generally the reason for such seasonal back-and-forth movement is to seek resources that aren’t available within a single area year-round.

But daily vertical movements by zooplankton in the ocean – upward by night to seek food, downward by day to escape predators – can also be considered migration. So can the movement of aphids when, having depleted the young leaves on one food plant, their offspring then fly onward to a different host plant, with no one aphid ever returning to where it started.

Dingle is an evolutionary biologist who studies insects. His definition is more intricate than Berger’s, citing those five features that distinguish migration from other forms of movement. They allow for the fact that, for example, aphids will become sensitive to blue light (from the sky) when it’s time for takeoff on their big journey, and sensitive to yellow light (reflected from tender young leaves) when it’s appropriate to land. Birds will fatten themselves with heavy feeding in advance of a long migrational flight. The value of his definition, Dingle argues, is that it focuses attention on what the phenomenon of wildebeest migration shares with the phenomenon of the aphids, and therefore helps guide researchers towards understanding how evolution has produced them all.

Human behaviour, however, is having a detrimental impact on animal migration. The pronghorn, which resembles an antelope, though they are unrelated, is the fastest land mammal of the New World. One population, which spends the summer in the mountainous Grand Teton National Park of the western USA, follows a narrow route from its summer range in the mountains, across a river, and down onto the plains. Here they wait out the frozen months, feeding mainly on sagebrush blown clear of snow. These pronghorns are notable for the invariance of their migration route and the severity of its constriction at three bottlenecks. If they can’t pass through each of the three during their spring migration, they can’t reach their bounty of summer grazing; if they can’t pass through again in autumn, escaping south onto those windblown plains, they are likely to die trying to overwinter in the deep snow. Pronghorn, dependent on distance vision and speed to keep safe from predators, traverse high, open shoulders of land, where they can see and run. At one of the bottlenecks, forested hills rise to form a V, leaving a corridor of open ground only about 150 metres wide, filled with private homes. Increasing development is leading toward a crisis for the pronghorn, threatening to choke off their passageway.

Conservation scientists, along with some biologists and land managers within the USA’s National Park Service and other agencies, are now working to preserve migrational behaviours, not just species and habitats. A National Forest has recognised the path of the pronghorn, much of which passes across its land, as a protected migration corridor. But neither the Forest Service nor the Park Service can control what happens on private land at a bottleneck. And with certain other migrating species, the challenge is complicated further – by vastly greater distances traversed, more jurisdictions, more borders, more dangers along the way. We will require wisdom and resoluteness to ensure that migrating species can continue their journeying a while longer.

questions.text: Local gulls and migrating arctic terns behave in the same way when offered food.
questions.question: Local gulls and migrating arctic terns behave in the same way when offered food.
questions.groupHtml:
Questions 14–18

Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage?

In boxes 14–18 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE  if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE  if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN  if there is no information on this
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Local gulls”, “migrating arctic terns”, “same way”, “offered food”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 2: “An arctic tern... will take no notice of a nice smelly herring offered... While local gulls will dive voraciously for such handouts, the tern flies on.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Khi được cho thức ăn, chim nhạn Bắc Cực ngó lơ (take no notice) và tiếp tục bay, trong khi mòng biển địa phương lại lao xuống ăn ngấu nghiến (dive voraciously). Do đó, hành vi của hai loài là hoàn toàn trái ngược nhau, không phải giống nhau (same way).

=> Đáp án: FALSE

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.text: Experts’ definitions of migration tend to vary according to their area of study.
questions.question: Experts’ definitions of migration tend to vary according to their area of study.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: *“Experts’ definitions”, “vary” (thay đổi/khác nhau), “according to their area of study” (theo lĩnh vực nghiên cứu)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 4: “But migration is a complex issue, and biologists define it differently, depending in part on what sorts of animals they study.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Đoạn văn khẳng định các nhà sinh vật học định nghĩa về di cư khác nhau (define it differently = vary), một phần phụ thuộc vào loại động vật mà họ nghiên cứu (what sorts of animals they study = area of study). Thông tin này hoàn toàn khớp với câu hỏi.

=> Đáp án: TRUE

questions.text: Very few experts agree that the movement of aphids can be considered migration.
questions.question: Very few experts agree that the movement of aphids can be considered migration.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Very few experts agree”, “movement of aphids”, “considered migration”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 5: “So can the movement of aphids... be considered migration.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Đoạn văn chỉ khẳng định sự di chuyển của rệp (aphids) cũng có thể được coi là di cư. Bài viết hoàn toàn không cung cấp thông tin về số lượng chuyên gia đồng ý hay phản đối nhận định này (very few experts agree hay nhiều).

=> Đáp án: NOT GIVEN

questions.text: Aphids’ journeys are affected by changes in the light that they perceive.
questions.question: Aphids’ journeys are affected by changes in the light that they perceive.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Aphids’ journeys”, “affected by”, “changes in the light”, “perceive” (nhận thức/cảm nhận)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 6: “...aphids will become sensitive to blue light (from the sky) when it's time for takeoff... and sensitive to yellow light (reflected from tender young leaves) when it's appropriate to land.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Hành trình của loài rệp chịu ảnh hưởng trực tiếp bởi ánh sáng: chúng nhạy cảm với ánh sáng xanh để cất cánh và nhạy cảm với ánh sáng vàng để hạ cánh. Việc thay đổi phản ứng theo các màu sắc ánh sáng khác nhau đồng nghĩa với việc hành trình của chúng bị tác động bởi sự thay đổi ánh sáng mà chúng cảm nhận được.

=> Đáp án: TRUE

questions.text: Dingles aim is to distinguish between the migratory behaviours of different species.
questions.question: Dingles aim is to distinguish between the migratory behaviours of different species.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Dingle's aim” (mục đích của Dingle), “distinguish” (phân biệt), “migratory behaviours of different species”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 6: “The value of his definition, Dingle argues, is that it focuses attention on what the phenomenon of wildebeest migration shares with the phenomenon of the aphids, and therefore helps guide researchers...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Định nghĩa của Dingle hướng sự chú ý vào điểm chung (shares with) giữa hiện tượng di cư của linh dương đầu bò và loài rệp để tìm ra cơ chế tiến hóa chung, chứ mục đích của ông không phải là để phân biệt (distinguish) sự khác nhau giữa các loài. Do đó câu hỏi đưa tin sai lệch so với bài đọc.

=> Đáp án: FALSE

questions.text:
According to Dingle, migratory routes are likely to
___________.
questions.question:
According to Dingle, migratory routes are likely to
___________.
questions.groupHtml:
Questions 19–22

Complete each sentence with the correct ending, A–G, below.

Write the correct letter in boxes 19–22 on your answer sheet.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “According to Dingle”, “migratory routes”, “likely to”
 follow a straight line.
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 1: “The biologist Hugh Dingle has identified five characteristics... they tend to be linear, not zigzaggy...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Theo Dingle, một trong các đặc điểm của tuyến đường di cư là chúng có xu hướng theo đường thẳng, không ngoằn ngoèo (linear, not zigzaggy). Cụm này đồng nghĩa với cụm “follow a straight line” (đáp án G).

=> Đáp án: G (follow a straight line)

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.
sentenceStems: According to Dingle, migratory routes are likely to
To prepare for migration, animals are likely to
During migration, animals are unlikely to
Arctic terns illustrate migrating animals’ ability to
featureOptions: travel on open land where they can look out for predators.
eat more than they need for immediate purposes.
be governed by the availability of water.
sentenceEndings: travel on open land where they can look out for predators.
eat more than they need for immediate purposes.
be governed by the availability of water.
featureStatements:
According to Dingle, migratory routes are likely to
To prepare for migration, animals are likely to
During migration, animals are unlikely to
Arctic terns illustrate migrating animals’ ability to

questions.questionHtml:
According to Dingle, migratory routes are likely to 19_______

To prepare for migration, animals are likely to 20_______

During migration, animals are unlikely to 21_______

Arctic terns illustrate migrating animals’ ability to 22_______

List of options

A. be discouraged by difficulties.

B. travel on open land where they can look out for predators.

C. eat more than they need for immediate purposes.

D. be repeated daily.

E. ignore distractions.

F. be governed by the availability of water.

G. follow a straight line.

questions.text:
To prepare for migration, animals are likely to
___________.
questions.question:
To prepare for migration, animals are likely to
___________.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “To prepare for migration”, “animals are likely to”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 1: “...they involve special behaviours concerning preparation (such as overfeeding) and arrival...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Hành vi đặc biệt liên quan đến việc chuẩn bị (preparation) được Dingle lấy ví dụ là ăn quá nhiều (overfeeding). Khái niệm này hoàn toàn khớp với đáp án C: “eat more than they need for immediate purposes” (ăn nhiều hơn mức nhu cầu phục vụ mục đích tức thời).

=> Đáp án: C(eat more than they need for immediate purposes)

questions.text:
During migration, animals are unlikely to
 ___________.
questions.question:
During migration, animals are unlikely to
 ___________.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “During migration”, “animals are unlikely to” (động vật không có khả năng/hiếm khi làm gì)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 1: “...which keeps them undistracted by temptations and undeterred by challenges that would turn other animals aside.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Những động vật di cư tập trung cao độ vào sứ mệnh lớn lao nên chúng sẽ không bị nản lòng trước các thử thách (undeterred by challenges). Vì câu hỏi dùng cấu trúc phủ định “unlikely to”, ta chọn đáp án A: “be discouraged by difficulties” (bị nản lòng bởi những khó khăn) – vì undeterred đồng nghĩa với not discouraged.

=> Đáp án: A ( be discouraged by difficulties)

questions.text:
Arctic terns illustrate migrating animals’ ability to
 ___________
questions.question:
Arctic terns illustrate migrating animals’ ability to
 ___________
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: *“Arctic terns”, “illustrate migrating animals’ ability to” (minh họa cho khả năng...)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 2: “The arctic tern resists distraction because it is driven at that moment by an instinctive sense of... larger purpose.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Loài chim nhạn Bắc Cực được đưa ra làm ví dụ chứng minh cho việc chúng có thể cưỡng lại các yếu tố gây xao nhãng (resists distraction) từ lời mời gọi thức ăn dọc đường để tiếp tục bay. Cụm này khớp hoàn toàn với đáp án E: “ignore distractions” (bỏ qua những sự xao nhãng).

=> Đáp án: E (ignore distractions)

questions.text: Pronghorns rely on their eyesight and 23 .................. to avoid predators.
questions.question: Pronghorns rely on their eyesight and 23 .................. to avoid predators.
questions.groupHtml:
Questions 23–26

Complete the summary below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Pronghorns rely on”, “eyesight”, “avoid predators”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 7: “Pronghorn, dependent on distance vision and speed to keep safe from predators, traverse high, open shoulders of land...”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Để giữ an toàn trước kẻ săn mồi (keep safe from predators = avoid predators), loài linh dương sừng gai phụ thuộc vào tầm nhìn xa (distance vision = eyesight) và yếu tố còn lại là tốc độ — speed.

=> Đáp án: speed

_blockConfig.noteContent:
Pronghorns rely on their eyesight and [23] to avoid predators. One particular population’s summer habitat is a national park, and their winter home is on the [24] where they go to avoid the danger presented by the snow at that time of year. However, their route between these two areas contains three [25] One problem is the construction of new homes in a narrow 
[26] corridor of land on the pronghorns’ route.

questions.questionHtml:
The migration of pronghorns

Pronghorns rely on their eyesight and 23_______ to avoid predators. One particular population’s summer habitat is a national park, and their winter home is on the 24_______ where they go to avoid the danger presented by the snow at that time of year. However, their route between these two areas contains three 25_______ One problem is the construction of new homes in a narrow

26_______ corridor of land on the pronghorns’ route.

questions.text: One particular population’s summer habitat is a national park, and their winter home is on the 24 .................
questions.question: One particular population’s summer habitat is a national park, and their winter home is on the 24 .................
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “winter home”, “avoid the danger presented by the snow”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 7: “...follows a narrow route from its summer range in the mountains... and down onto the plains. Here they wait out the frozen months, feeding mainly on sagebrush blown clear of snow.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Nơi chúng chọn làm nhà để đi xuống trú ẩn và vượt qua những tháng băng giá đông lạnh (wait out the frozen months = winter home) nhằm tránh tuyết dày chính là các vùng đồng bằng — plains.

=> Đáp án: plains

questions.text: However, their route between these two areas contains three
questions.question: However, their route between these two areas contains three
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “route between these two areas”, “contains three”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 7: “These pronghorn are notable for the invariance of their migration route and the severity of its construction at three bottlenecks.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Tuyến đường di cư của chúng có đặc điểm là cực kỳ khắc nghiệt tại cấu trúc gồm có 3 điểm nghẽn giao thông — bottlenecks.

=> Đáp án: bottlenecks

questions.question: The wildebeest migration is the largest in the world.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “construction of new homes”, “narrow... of land”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 7: “...leaving a corridor of open ground only about 150 metres wide, filled with private homes.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Việc xây dựng các ngôi nhà riêng (private homes = new homes) xuất hiện dày đặc tại một dải đất trống hẹp chỉ rộng khoảng 150 mét. Danh từ chỉ dải đất/hành lang này là corridor (hoặc từ đồng nghĩa xuất hiện trong đoạn là passageway).

=> Đáp án: corridor
